execution_environment: LOCAL residential IP only (stats.wnba.com/stats.nba.com block datacenter IPs). Not run in CI or agent VM.

tracking.player_frame_selection: the returned frame that contains a player id column (personId or PLAYER_ID) and the most columns
tracking.player_id_column: personId (V3) or PLAYER_ID (legacy) - whichever is present is retained

GAME_ID.source: injected by collector from the requested game_id

tracking.field_set_note: The full column set is endpoint-defined (nba_api). representative_signal_fields is a non-exhaustive contract of the key playmaking/usage signals; the collector retains the endpoint superset.

hustle.player_frame_selection: the returned frame that contains a DEFLECT* column and a player id column (PLAYER_ID or personId)
hustle.player_id_column: PLAYER_ID or personId - whichever is present is retained

GAME_ID.source: injected by collector from the requested game_id

hustle.field_set_note: Full column set is endpoint-defined; representative_signal_fields is a non-exhaustive contract of the key defensive-intensity/rebounding signals.

normalization_rules: GAME_ID is stored as a string on every row.
The player id column is whichever of (personId, PLAYER_ID) the endpoint returns; it is retained verbatim, not renamed.
Numeric endpoint fields are retained with their native dtypes; no unit conversion is applied by the collector.
duplicate_resolution: On flush the collector drop_duplicates on [GAME_ID, player_id_column]. Re-running is resumable: already-saved GAME_IDs are skipped, and re-merged frames are de-duplicated on the same key (last write for a given key wins within a merge).
missing_value_semantics: Endpoint omissions are retained as NaN. A game whose response has no valid player frame is skipped and logged (never written as an empty/garbage row).
primary_key_note: Uniqueness is per (GAME_ID, player). The collector helper _dedup_cols returns exactly [GAME_ID, <player id column present>].
